mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust economic activity and corporate hiring, leading to upward revisions in aggregate earnings expectations and positively driving S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market is historically correlated with bull markets.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period, or the hit rate of positive returns following positive signals is statistically indistinguishable from 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, it triggers expectations of future monetary policy tightening by the Federal Reserve. This increases the discount rate on future earnings, negatively impacting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPI is above 3%, the S&P 500 exhibits positive average 60-day forward returns, or the signal's hit rate is below 50% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and an increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer health. This interaction signals a high probability of imminent recession and negative corporate earnings surprises, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2 AND 3-month avg of MoM RSAFS growth < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a statistically significant negative return profile (t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods where the trigger condition is not met.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a marked deceleration in the year-over-year growth of industrial production (INDPRO) serves as a leading indicator of peaking economic activity and potential recession, negatively impacting investor sentiment and forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Conference Board Leading Economic Index.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO YoY growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive or statistically insignificant during periods identified as late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) represents the risk-free rate. A higher level, ceteris paribus, increases the discount rate applied to corporate cash flows, reducing the present value of equities and exerting negative pressure on short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard dividend discount models are inversely related to the risk-free rate.
failure_condition: A regression of 5-day S&P 500 returns on the level of FEDFUNDS shows a positive or statistically insignificant coefficient over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.5 percentage points above its 12-month low (a variant of the Sahm Rule), it reliably signals the start of a recession, triggering a significant sell-off in equities.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a trigger event, the S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 40% of historical instances, indicating the signal is not a reliable predictor of a downturn.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 2.5%), the combination of accelerating housing starts (HOUST) and strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates powerful wealth effects and consumer confidence, driving higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing wealth is a major component of household net worth.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the joint signal from HOUST and RSAFS momentum fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a hit rate statistically greater than 50% (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects an expansion in the overall economy, which directly translates into higher aggregate corporate earnings. This fundamental growth supports higher equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:S&P 500 aggregate earnings growth is highly correlated with GDP growth.
failure_condition: Over a full business cycle, the correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.
